description: The ImageColorDistributionGraph component is used to make mesh data for a set of points with colors and sizes based on a provided image's pixels. The mesh needs to be used with Billboard geometry to be viewed properly.

fields.Texture: The texture to analyze and use to generate the vertex graph for this mesh.
fields.ColorSpace: The color space to use for X, Y, and Z coordinates on the graph for.
fields.MaxTextureSize: The max size of the image on one axis that this image will use when making the graph. The image gets resized internally to fit this size.
fields.BaseSize: The size of any color point on the graph at minimum.
fields.AccumulateSize: How much to add to the size of a graph point if a color is repeated with the same RGB values.
fields.MaxSize: The maximum size a color point on the graph can be.
fields.Scale: How much to scale the graph up or down.
fields.AlphaThreshold: if alpha is below this threshold, then don't add the color to the graph.

usage: Attach this component to a slot, and place it into a Mesh Renderer Component with an Unlit Material set to use billboard geometry to view the mesh and its graph points.
